articles.title: Dow closes little changed after touching record; Nasdaq slides to start July
articles.summary: S&P 500 fell 0.22% to 7,483.23; Nasdaq dropped 0.66% to 26,040.03 as semiconductor stocks sold off. Dow slipped 0.03% to 52,305.24 despite touching an intraday record. Meta surged 9% on cloud business plans. Russell 2000 hit all-time high intraday. Great Rotation from tech to Dow/value continued. ADP payrolls +98K vs 110K expected. Warsh spoke at ECB forum, stuck to 2% target. Oil fell on Iran talks progress.
articles.extracted_text: Full CNBC market wrap covering: closing levels for all major indices; semiconductor selloff with MU -10%, SNDK -10%; Meta +9-10% on cloud plans; GIS +6% on earnings beat; NKE fell on China weakness; SSTK -28% on Getty merger collapse; CRM/NOW upgrades; CAT -7%, WMT -5.3%; ADP +98K miss; Fed Warsh speaking; Iran talks. Russell 2000 hit intraday all-time high; 65.7% NYSE advancers despite tech drag.

articles.title: Stock Market Today (July 1, 2026): Dow, S&P 500 rise to start Q3 2026
articles.summary: Major U.S. indexes opened Q3 with mixed action. Dow and S&P 500 pushed higher midday driven by comm services and financials; Nasdaq in red on tech selloff. Meta +8.1%, General Mills +8.8%. CoreWeave -12.75%, Nebius -13.64%, Corning -10.4%. Kroger -2.8% on Giant Eagle acquisition. WTI -1.1% to $68.77, Brent -1% to $72.20. US-Iran talks in Doha. USMCA trade deal under review.
articles.extracted_text: Midday market report covering: Dow +0.37%, S&P 500 +0.15%, Russell +0.59% midday; 65.7% advancers. Big movers: GIS +8.8%, META +8.1%, APP +7.6%, CRWV -12.75%, NBIS -13.64%, GLW -10.4%. Kroger $1.65B Giant Eagle deal. SpaceX initiated Outperform by Wedbush. ADP +98K miss. Warsh spoke at Sintra, no forward guidance. US-Iran talks in Doha. Strait of Hormuz ship incident. USMCA review.

articles.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq edge lower as tech shares slide
articles.summary: U.S. stocks closed slightly lower as tech weakness offset Meta gains. Fed Chair Warsh stuck firmly to 2% target, said he'd disappoint anyone expecting loose policy. Brent fell 1.89% to $71.57, WTI -1.32% to $68.58 on Iran peace progress. Gold jumped >2% to $4,071 after soft ADP data. 10Y yield rose to ~4.465%. Oil inventories fell 3.8M barrels. DXY rose.
articles.extracted_text: Closing wrap: S&P 500 and Nasdaq lower on tech/semi weakness; Meta +7% support. Warsh: sticking to 2% firmly, no forward guidance, incorporating real-time data in 9-12 months, dot plot stays. ADP miss supportive of gold. Iran talks in Doha; Strait of Hormuz ship incident; Brent -1.89% to $71.57, WTI -1.32% to $68.58. Gold +2% to $4,071. EIA crude draw 3.8M barrels. 10Y yield 4.465%.

articles.title: Stock Market Live July 1, 2026: S&P 500 Lower as Investors Wait on the Fed
articles.summary: Markets opened Q3 lower. Top gainers: COIN +8.93%, META +8.81%, GIS +8.53%, PLTR +7.77%, ELV +7.62%. Top losers: GLW -13.62%, KLAC -11.77%, TER -11.68%, MU -10.57%, AMAT -9.97%. Nike fell despite beating EPS by $0.59. Evercore reiterated Outperform on NVDA. 10Y yield at 4.48%.
articles.extracted_text: Closing levels: S&P 500 7,491 (-0.11%), Dow 52,356.70 (+0.04%), Nasdaq 100 29,865.80 (-1.39%), Russell 2K 3,013.23 (-0.35%). Gainers/losers tables. COIN +8.93%, META +8.81%, GIS +8.53%. GLW -13.62%, KLAC -11.77%, TER -11.68%, MU -10.57%, AMAT -9.97%. NKE earnings beat but China sales weakness. Evercore NVDA Outperform. Warsh ECB speech.

articles.title: Stocks Lower Early as Focus Turns to Warsh
articles.summary: Stocks pulled back early after strong Q2. ADP +98K vs 112K consensus. Challenger job cuts 46K in June (-53% MoM). JOLTS 7.6M openings Tuesday. Fed Chair Warsh speaking at ECB forum. WTI $68.74 early. 10Y yield 4.48%. Chip stocks sold off after Tuesday rally; AI ROI concerns. June payrolls due Thursday (110K expected vs 172K May).
articles.extracted_text: Early session wrap: ADP +98K miss, Challenger 46K cuts (-53% MoM), JOLTS 7.6M. Warsh ECB speech at 9am. 10Y 4.48%. WTI $68.74. DXY 101.51. VIX 16.85. Chip stocks -80% YTD, Mag 7 negative returns. AI price war concerns. SNDK -4%, MU -3%, MRVL -2% pre-market. NKE -2%, BE +8%, STZ +1%, AA -5%, KR -2%. June payrolls Thursday: 110K expected.

articles.title: Stock Market News for July 1, 2026
articles.summary: Q2 recap: Dow +12.9% (best since Q4 2022), S&P 500 +14.9%, Nasdaq +21.4% (both best since Q2 2020). H1 2026: Dow +8.9%, S&P +9.6%, Nasdaq +12.0%. Tuesday's chip rally: AMD +7.7%, SNDK +10.9%. VIX fell 6.8% to 16.45. Iran ceasefire boosted sentiment.
articles.extracted_text: Q2/H1 context: Dow +12.9% Q2, S&P +14.9% Q2, Nasdaq +21.4% Q2. H1: Dow +8.9%, S&P +9.6%, Nasdaq +12.0%. Tuesday close: Dow 52,319.20 (+0.3%), S&P 7,499.36 (+0.8%), Nasdaq 26,213.72 (+1.5%). VIX 16.45 (-6.8%). Tuesday chip rally context. US-Iran ceasefire announcement Sunday. Earnings season starts mid-July.

articles.title: Chip Stocks Slide as Semiconductor Index Tumbles
articles.summary: Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) fell 3.6% in early trading. MU -6.7%, ARM -4.6%, INTC -4.0%, AMD -3.5%, TSM -3.4%, MRVL -2.7%, NVDA -2.3%, AVGO -1.4%. Profit-taking after strong H1. AI capex sustainability concerns.
articles.extracted_text: SOX -3.6% led by MU -6.7%, ARM -4.6%, INTC -4%, AMD -3.5%, TSM -3.4%, MRVL -2.7%, NVDA -2.3%, AVGO -1.4%. Analyst: market taking breather after strong run. AI capex growth concerns. Memory price headwinds (DRAM/NAND). Intel execution challenges. Broadcom relative resilience due to diversified model.

articles.title: Meta Is Planning a Cloud Business to Sell AI Computing Power
articles.summary: Meta Platforms is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business to sell access to AI computing power and models, setting up competition with AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud. Stock surged 9-10% on the news.
articles.extracted_text: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI compute. Debating whether to offer hosted AI models or raw compute. Stock surged ~9% to $612.91. Reframes Meta's massive capex as potential revenue stream. Positions Meta against AWS, Azure, Google Cloud.

articles.title: Meta stock pops on cloud push to sell excess AI compute power
articles.summary: Meta shares popped on news that the company is building out a new cloud business to sell excess computing power to outside customers. CNBC confirmed the Bloomberg report.
articles.extracted_text: CNBC confirmation of Bloomberg report. Meta building cloud business to monetize AI infrastructure. Stock reaction: +9-10%.

articles.title: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI capacity
articles.summary: Reuters confirms Meta Platforms is building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing capacity, as tech giants seek returns on massive AI investments.
articles.extracted_text: Reuters confirmation: Meta building cloud business to sell excess AI compute capacity. Part of broader trend of tech giants seeking ROI on AI infrastructure spending.

articles.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI falls more than expected in June
articles.summary: June U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI: 53.3 vs. 53.9 consensus and 54.0 in May. Sixth consecutive month in expansion but decelerating.
articles.extracted_text: ISM Manufacturing PMI: 53.3 actual vs 53.9 consensus, down from 54.0 in May. Sixth straight expansion month. New orders and production still growing but at slower pace. Prices index moderated.

articles.title: Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%; June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI Report
articles.summary: ISM Manufacturing PMI registered 53.3% for June 2026, down 0.7 points from May's 54.0%. Sixth consecutive month of expansion.
articles.extracted_text: Official ISM release: June PMI 53.3% (-0.7pp from 54.0% May). New Orders Index 54.1%, Production Index 55.0%, Employment Index 50.8%, Prices Index 52.1% (-3.5pp). Six straight months above 50. Business Survey panelists confident in production and revenue growth.

articles.title: Constellation Brands Q1 fiscal 2027 earnings beat
articles.summary: Constellation Brands Q1 fiscal 2027: adj. EPS $3.43 vs $3.20 est., revenue $2.43B vs $2.39B est. Revenue fell 3.3% YoY but topped estimates. Stock +4-5% after hours.
articles.extracted_text: STZ Q1 FY2027: adj EPS $3.43 beat $3.20-3.27 consensus. Net sales $2.43B beat $2.39B. Revenue -3.3% YoY. Conference call July 1. After-hours: +5.08% to $143.59.

articles.title: Gold Prices Rise as Weak US Jobs Data Boosts Fed Cut Expectations
articles.summary: Gold staged a remarkable intraday reversal from below $4,000 to above $4,100 after weak ADP data. Spot gold closed around $4,071, up >2%. Silver, platinum, palladium also advanced.
articles.extracted_text: Gold rallied from sub-$4,000 to above $4,100 intraday, settling ~$4,071 (+2%). ADP miss of 98K vs 110K reignited rate cut hopes. Warsh comments on easing inflation risks supported. Silver +0.96% to $58.74. Gold-to-silver ratio steady.

articles.title: ADP Missed. Gold Shrugged. Warsh Is Live in Sintra Right Now.
articles.summary: Gold holding near $4,040, up 0.8% despite ADP miss. Market looking past jobs data to Warsh's ECB speech. If Warsh signals hawkish stance, gold downside; if dovish, further rally.
articles.extracted_text: Gold $4,040 +0.8% at time of Warsh speech. ADP +98K vs 122K prior and 110K forecast. Market awaiting Warsh tone at Sintra.

articles.title: US ISM Manufacturing PMI - Forex Factory
articles.summary: US ISM Manufacturing PMI: July 1, 2026 actual 53.3. Next release August 3, 2026.
